requirements.title: Keyword Namespace Separation
requirements.description: The language separates keywords from ordinary identifiers.
requirements.notes: This avoids collisions between compiler-recognized syntax words and user-defined names.

children.title: Hash-Prefixed Keywords
children.description: Keywords in source code begin with `#` so that words without the prefix remain available as identifiers.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Source code can use a plain word as an identifier even when the corresponding `#`-prefixed form is treated as a keyword.

requirements.title: Builtin Symbol Exposure
requirements.description: The language exposes compiler-provided builtin types and functions through explicit source-level bindings.
requirements.notes: Builtin symbols should follow the same namespace-preserving design direction as `#`-prefixed keywords, so compiler-provided names do not silently occupy ordinary identifiers.

children.title: Explicit Builtin Binding
children.description: Builtin types and functions are made available to user programs only when source code explicitly binds them.

acceptanceCriteria.description: A builtin symbol is not available under an ordinary identifier unless the source code binds it explicitly.
Source code can bind a builtin symbol to an ordinary identifier through a `#`-prefixed builtin-binding construct.

children.title: Standard Library Primitive Exports
children.description: The standard library exposes selected primitive builtin symbols through its public module surface.

acceptanceCriteria.description: The standard library provides a public binding for the builtin type `i32`.
The standard library provides public bindings for the builtin arithmetic functions `i32_add`, `i32_sub`, `i32_mul`, `i32_div`, and `i32_mod`.

children.notes: This captures the externally visible standard-library surface without constraining the internal module layout used to organize those bindings.

requirements.title: Package Manifest
requirements.description: A package is described by a manifest file that identifies the package and its Felis library entrypoint.
requirements.notes: This captures the externally visible package contract without constraining unrelated manifest fields or package-specific values.

children.title: Manifest Name And Entrypoint Fields
children.description: A package manifest declares the package name and the Felis library entrypoint.

acceptanceCriteria.description: A package can provide a `neco-package.json` manifest with a `name` field.
A package can provide a `neco-package.json` manifest with a `felis-lib-entrypoint` field.

requirements.title: Module Visibility
requirements.description: Source code can explicitly expose modules and symbols through a module's public surface.
requirements.notes: This captures externally observable module visibility behavior without constraining unrelated name resolution details.

children.title: Public Module Declarations
children.description: Source code can declare a child module as public.

acceptanceCriteria.description: A `#pub #mod` declaration makes the declared child module part of the enclosing module's public surface.

children.title: Public Re-Exports
children.description: Source code can expose an existing symbol as public from the current module.

acceptanceCriteria.description: A `#pub #use` declaration makes the referenced symbol part of the current module's public surface.
